title: Chill Out Day: National Relaxation Day
story: Every August 15, people across the country celebrate National Relaxation Day by doing absolutely nothing productive! This special holiday encourages everyone to take a break from busy schedules and stress. People celebrate by sleeping in, reading books, taking bubble baths, or lounging in hammocks. Some folks turn off their phones and computers for the whole day. Others enjoy peaceful activities like meditation, gentle yoga, or listening to calm music. The goal is simple: slow down and recharge your batteries!
activity_question: What do people often turn off during National Relaxation Day?
activity_choices: All the lights in their house
follow_up_question: What's your favorite way to relax when you're feeling stressed or tired?

title: Freedom Flags and Sweet Celebrations
story: Every August 15, over a billion people in India celebrate their country's independence with incredible pride! Streets fill with parades featuring colorful floats, marching bands, and dancers in traditional costumes. Families gather to watch the Prime Minister raise the national flag and give a speech from the Red Fort in Delhi. People fly kites in the colors of the Indian flag—orange, white, and green—while enjoying sweet treats and sharing stories of freedom.
activity_question: What colors are featured on the Indian flag that people use for kite flying?
follow_up_question: What would you include in your own independence day celebration?

title: Acadian Pride Day Celebration
story: Every August 15, Acadians around the world celebrate their unique French heritage with National Acadian Day! These descendants of French settlers who came to North America centuries ago gather for festivals filled with traditional music, dancing, and delicious foods like rappie pie and fricot. Communities raise the colorful Acadian flag with its blue, white, red stripes and golden star, while families share stories passed down through generations.
activity_question: What do Acadians raise on National Acadian Day to show their pride?
activity_choices: The Acadian flag with blue, white, red stripes and a golden star
activity_answer: The Acadian flag with blue, white, red stripes and a golden star
bonus_fact: The Acadian flag's golden star represents the Virgin Mary, their patron saint.
